title: fagraea resinosa
intro: Fagraea resinosa, also known as the 'Frangipani tree' or 'Strangler Fig', is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. Fagraea resinosa is believed to have various health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in Southeast Asia.
